name: Argentina

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa (Visa de Turista)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals whose nationality requires a visa to enter Argentina as tourists. Permits leisure travel, family visits, and short-stay tourism.
visa_types.notes: Required only for nationalities not covered by Argentina's visa exemption list. Many nationalities (Canada, USA, EU, Australia, etc.) enter visa-free for up to 90 days. Fee varies by consulate and reciprocity agreements. Application submitted to Argentine consulate or embassy in country of residence.

visa_types.name: Transitory Business Visa (Visa de Negocios Transitoria)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals conducting short-term business activities in Argentina, such as commercial negotiations, market studies, meetings, and business representation.
visa_types.notes: Permits business activities but not paid employment in Argentina. Separate from the temporary work visa categories. Applicable to nationals requiring a visa. Fee varies by consulate.

visa_types.name: Transitory Visa for Technicians, Professionals and Artists
visa_types.purpose: For foreign technicians, professionals, and artists performing short-term skilled work or artistic activities in Argentina for up to 30 days.
visa_types.notes: Short-term work authorization for technicians, professionals, and performing artists. For longer-term work, the Temporary Work Visa (23E or 23A) applies.

visa_types.name: Temporary Work Visa – Intra-Company Transfer (Category 23E / Personal de Traslado)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign employees transferred intra-company to work in Argentina under a multinational corporate arrangement.
visa_types.notes: Category 23E under Argentine immigration law. Fee is USD 250 (USD 150 base + USD 100 supplemental). Requires PIM (Entry Permit / Permiso de Ingreso) from DNM, valid RENURE, apostilled police clearance with Spanish translation, company incorporation certificate, work authorization document. Stay duration as per Entry Permit. Multiple entry. 60-day travel insurance required.

visa_types.name: Temporary Work Visa – Migrant Worker (Category 23A / Trabajador Migrante)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who have obtained a work contract in Argentina and wish to reside and work there on a temporary basis.
visa_types.notes: Category 23A under Argentine immigration law. Fee is USD 250 (USD 150 base + USD 100 supplemental). Requires original notarized and legalized work contract (Contrato de Trabajo), PIM from DNM, apostilled police clearance with Spanish translation, and travel insurance. Stay duration as per Entry Permit. Multiple entry.

visa_types.name: Transitory Student Visa (Visa de Estudiante Transitoria)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign students enrolled in academic programs, exchange programs, internships, scholarships, or cultural exchange activities in Argentina for up to 12 months.
visa_types.notes: Covers full-time students, international mobility students, cultural exchange volunteers, exchange program participants, interns, and scholarship holders per Directive 2802/2023. Valid up to 12 months (2 years for official education system). Requires PRECARGA / RENURE registration from Argentine educational institution, apostilled police clearance (for stays over 6 months if applicant is over 16), university acceptance letter, and proof of financial means. Application recommended at least 45 days before travel. Consular interview required after documentation approval.

visa_types.name: Temporary Student Visa (Category 23J)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals pursuing long-term academic studies at Argentine universities or tertiary institutions requiring a full temporary residence classification.
visa_types.notes: Category 23J under Argentine immigration law. Fee is USD 250 (USD 150 base + USD 100 supplemental). Requires university acceptance letter with syllabus, academic certificates, PRECARGA from university, 6 months bank statements, sponsor details with income tax returns (3 years), and travel insurance covering full stay. Stay duration as per Entry Permit. Multiple entry.

visa_types.name: Digital Nomad Transitional Residence (Residencia Transitoria como Nómada Digital)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign remote workers who provide services via digital or telecommunications means to clients or companies domiciled abroad, allowing extended stays in Argentina.
visa_types.notes: Established by DNM Disposition No. 758/2022. Available to nationals of countries that do not require a tourist visa to enter Argentina. Duration: 180 days, renewable once for an additional 180 days (total up to 12 months). Requires signed application, CV, proof of remote work activity (contracts, company endorsement, income receipts), and at least one professional reference. In-person application only; not available via the RADEX online system. Includes perks such as discounts on transportation, hotels, coworking spaces, and cultural activities. Standard migration fee applies.

visa_types.name: Seaman Visa (Visa de Marinero)
visa_types.purpose: For maritime seafarers visiting Argentine ports as part of their employment aboard a vessel.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. Maximum stay is 10 days. Stamp validity up to 3 months. Two pathways: (1) E-Visa processed entirely by Argentina's Immigration Authority without consulate involvement; (2) Sticker Visa processed at the consulate. Sticker visa requires 14 documents including Spanish invitation letter from Argentine shipping company, Permiso de Ingreso from Immigration, original employment contract, and Seaman's book (CDC). For crew members on active maritime duties only.

visa_types.name: Transitory Religious Visa (Visa Religiosa Transitoria)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals invited by a religious congregation in Argentina to participate in religious activities or missions.
visa_types.notes: Requires invitation letter from Argentine congregation certified by Argentina's Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Maximum stay up to 30 days, multiple entry. Stamp validity up to 3 months. Fee: USD 200. Requires cover letter on congregation's letterhead, Spanish translation, round-trip flight tickets (dummy acceptable), hotel reservation (dummy acceptable), and travel insurance covering entire stay.

visa_types.name: Temporary Religious Visa (Category 23G)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign religious workers assigned to serve with a religious congregation in Argentina on a longer-term basis.
visa_types.notes: Category 23G under Argentine immigration law. Fee is USD 150. Requires apostilled police clearance with Spanish translation, congregation letterhead letter, flight reservations, and 60-day travel insurance. Stay up to 1 year, multiple entry.

visa_types.name: AVE – Electronic Travel Authorization (Autorización de Viaje Electrónica)
visa_types.purpose: An electronic travel authorization (e-visa equivalent) that allows foreign nationals who hold a valid US visa or ESTA to enter Argentina without applying for a full consular visa.
visa_types.notes: Available to any nationality that would otherwise require an Argentine consular visa, provided they hold a valid US visa (B2, J, B1, O, P (P1-P2-P3), E, or H-1B) with at least 110 days remaining validity, or a current ESTA, or have had at least one US entry within the past 2 years. Cost: USD 400. Processed in 20 business days by National Directorate of Migrations. For tourism purposes only. Payment receipt does not guarantee entry - approval email required before travel. One 90-day extension available in-country. Indian, Chinese (PRC), and Dominican Republic nationals with qualifying US credentials may be exempt from the AVE fee entirely under 2025 resolutions.

visa_types.name: Temporary Residence as Investor (Residencia Temporaria como Inversionista)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals making a qualifying productive, commercial, or service investment in Argentina, granting temporary residence leading to permanent residence.
visa_types.notes: Minimum investment of ARS 1,500,000 in a productive, commercial, or service activity (amount subject to inflation erosion; verify current threshold with official source). Temporary residence granted for 1 year, renewable up to 3 years. After 2 years of residence and maintaining investment, holders become eligible for citizenship; at year 3 eligible for permanent residency. Separate tracks for MERCOSUR and non-MERCOSUR nationals. No sector restriction.

visa_types.name: Temporary Residence as Rentista / Pensionado (Passive Income Residence)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals with regular passive income from abroad (pensions, annuities, dividends, rents) who wish to reside in Argentina.
visa_types.notes: For foreign nationals with regular passive income from abroad such as pensions, annuities, dividends, or rental income. Temporary residence for up to 2 years, renewable. Specific minimum income amount not publicly confirmed on official page. Path to permanent residency after 3 years, citizenship after 2 years of residence.

visa_types.name: Citizenship by Investment (Ciudadanía por Inversión - Decreto 524/2025)
visa_types.purpose: Direct citizenship grant for foreign nationals making a 'relevant' investment in Argentina, as defined by the Ministry of Economy, with no prior residency requirement.
visa_types.notes: Established by Decreto 524/2025, signed by President Javier Milei on July 30, 2025. Amends Citizenship Law No. 346 by adding Article 2° bis. No prior residency required. Minimum investment threshold and eligible sectors deferred to Ministry of Economy regulation - not yet published as of research date. Processing: 30 business days from receipt of Agency report by the National Directorate of Migration. Security clearance required from Ministry of Security, UIF (financial intelligence unit), and SIDE (intelligence agency). Applications submitted to the Agency for Citizenship by Investment Programs under the Ministry of Economy.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Official / Service Passport Visa Exemption
visa_types.purpose: Visa-free entry for holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports from eligible countries on official missions to Argentina.
visa_types.notes: Holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports from a broad list of countries are exempt from visa requirements for official missions. Exempt countries include Albania, Algeria, Andorra, Angola, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Austria, Barbados, Belarus, Belize, Belgium, Bolivia, Botswana, Brazil, Bulgaria, Chile, China (up to 30 days), Colombia, Costa Rica, Croatia, Cuba, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Dominican Republic, Ecuador, Egypt, El Salvador, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Georgia, Greece, Grenada, Guatemala, Guyana, Haiti, Holy See, Honduras, Hong Kong, Hungary, India, Indonesia, Iceland, Israel, Italy, Jamaica, Japan, Kazakhstan, Korea, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malaysia, Malta, S.M.O. of Malta, Macedonia, Mexico, Monaco, Montenegro, Morocco, Mozambique, Netherlands, New Zealand, Nicaragua, Norway, Pakistan, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Russia, Saint Lucia, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, San Marino, Serbia, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, South Africa, Suriname, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Thailand, Trinidad and Tobago, Tunisia, Turkey, Ukraine, UAE, United Kingdom, Uruguay, Vatican, Venezuela, and Vietnam. China limited to 30 days. UN Laissez-Passer holders require a visa regardless of nationality.

visa_types.name: MERCOSUR Nationality Visa (Visa por Nacionalidad MERCOSUR)
visa_types.purpose: Facilitates temporary establishment in Argentina for nationals of MERCOSUR member and associate states under the MERCOSUR Residency Agreement framework.
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of MERCOSUR member or associate states: Brazil, Paraguay, Uruguay, Bolivia, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Guyana, Peru, and Suriname. Provides temporary establishment for up to 2 years. Requires valid passport (6+ months validity, 2 blank pages), photographs, application form, proof of address, certificate of good conduct (age 16+), and consular fee (USD/EUR 200 equivalent). Consular interview required.

visa_free.notes: No tourist visa required; MERCOSUR member state
No tourist visa required; MERCOSUR member state
No tourist visa required; MERCOSUR associate state
No tourist visa required; 30-day maximum stay
New RAEHK (Hong Kong SAR) passport holders only; 90 days visa-free
British National Overseas (BNO) passport holders; 30-day maximum stay
No tourist visa required; 30-day maximum stay
No tourist visa required; 30-day maximum stay
No tourist visa required; 30-day maximum stay
No tourist visa required; 30-day maximum stay
No tourist visa required; MERCOSUR member state
No tourist visa required for tourism; technical workers require a Technical Visa
No tourist visa required; MERCOSUR member state
No tourist visa required; listed as 'Vatican City State' on official Toronto consulate exemption page
No tourist visa required; 60-day maximum stay

visa_policy.notes: Argentina grants visa-free entry for most stays of up to 90 days. Venezuela is limited to 60 days. Grenada, Jamaica, Kazakhstan, Malaysia, and Macao are limited to 30 days. Hong Kong RAEHK passport holders receive 90 days; BNO passport holders receive 30 days. Vatican City State (Holy See) holders receive 90 days. Special exemptions exist for Indian, Chinese, and Dominican Republic nationals who hold a valid US visa or Green Card (conditional_access). The AVE (Electronic Travel Authorization) is available for eligible nationals holding US visas or ESTA. Singapore is visa-free for tourism; a Technical Visa is required for technical work activities.

credential.subtype: B2, J, B1, O, P (P1-P2-P3), E, or H-1B

conditional_access.conditions: Indian national must hold a valid US tourist or business/tourist visa (categories B2, J, B1, O, P (P1-P2-P3), E, or H-1B) for the entire stay. No Argentine visa or AVE required for tourism. AVE fee waived. Extendable once for additional 90 days. Per Resolution 353/2025 of the Chief of the Cabinet of Ministers.

Indian national must hold a valid US Permanent Resident Card (Green Card). Permitted entry categories: Tourist and Businesspersons. No consular visa required. Effective January 15, 2026. Extendable once for additional 90 days in-country subject to evaluation by the National Directorate of Migration.

credential.subtype: B1/B2, B2, J, B1, O, P (P1-P2-P3), E, or H-1B

conditional_access.conditions: Chinese (PRC) national must hold a valid US visa in a category equivalent to tourism or business (B1/B2, B2, J, B1, O, P (P1-P2-P3), E, or H-1B). No Argentine visa or AVE required for tourism and business. Authorized stay may be extended once for a similar period subject to evaluation by the National Directorate of Migration. Beneficiaries cannot change immigration category during stay. Per Resolution 316/2025 of the Office of the Chief of the Cabinet of Ministers, published July 22, 2025.

Chinese (PRC) national must hold a valid US Permanent Resident Card (Green Card). Permitted entry categories: Tourist and Businesspersons. No consular visa required. Effective January 15, 2026. Authorized stay may be extended once for a similar period subject to evaluation by the National Directorate of Migration.

credential.subtype: B1/B2, B2, J, B1, O, P (P1-P2-P3), E, or H-1B

conditional_access.conditions: Dominican Republic national must hold a valid US visa in a category equivalent to tourism or business (B1/B2, B2, J, B1, O, P (P1-P2-P3), E, or H-1B). No Argentine visa or AVE required for tourism. Authorized stay may be extended once subject to National Directorate of Migration approval. No change of immigration category permitted during stay. Per Resolution 316/2025 of the Office of the Chief of the Cabinet of Ministers, effective July 22, 2025.

Dominican Republic national must hold a valid US Permanent Resident Card (Green Card). Permitted entry categories: Tourist and Businesspersons. Permanent residence cards issued by the United States are deemed valid and sufficient to authorize and facilitate entry into the National Territory without the need for a consular visa. Effective January 15, 2026.

credential.subtype: B2, J, B1, O, P (P1-P2-P3), E, or H-1B

conditional_access.conditions: Any nationality requiring an Argentine visa may apply for the AVE (Autorización de Viaje Electrónica / Electronic Travel Authorization) instead of a consular visa, provided they hold a valid US visa in categories B2, J, B1, O, P (P1-P2-P3), E, or H-1B and the visa remains valid for at least 110 days. Cost: USD 400. Valid for 90 days from issue, multiple entries. Processed within 20 business days by the National Directorate of Migrations. For tourism purposes only. Payment receipt does not guarantee entry - approval email required before travel. One 90-day extension available in-country.

Any nationality requiring an Argentine visa may apply for the AVE provided they hold a current ESTA (US Electronic System for Travel Authorization), or have had at least one US entry within the past 2 years. Cost: USD 400. Valid for 90 days from issue, multiple entries. Processed within 20 business days. For tourism purposes only.

Holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports from a broad list of countries do not require visas for official missions to Argentina. List includes Albania, Algeria, Andorra, Angola, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Austria, Barbados, Belarus, Belize, Belgium, Bolivia, Botswana, Brazil, Bulgaria, Chile, People's Republic of China (up to 30 days), Colombia, Costa Rica, Croatia, Cuba, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Dominican Republic, Ecuador, Egypt, El Salvador, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Georgia, Greece, Grenada, Guatemala, Guyana, Haiti, Holy See, Honduras, Hong Kong, Hungary, India, Indonesia, Iceland, Israel, Italy, Jamaica, Japan, Kazakhstan, Korea, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malaysia, Malta, S.M.O. of Malta, Macedonia, Mexico, Monaco, Montenegro, Morocco, Mozambique, The Netherlands, New Zealand, Nicaragua, Norway, Pakistan, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, The Philippines, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Russian Federation, Saint Lucia, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, San Marino, Serbia, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, South Africa, Suriname, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Thailand, Trinidad and Tobago, Tunisia, Turkey, Ukraine, United Arab Emirates, United Kingdom, Uruguay, Vatican, Venezuela, and Vietnam. China limited to 30 days. UN Laissez-Passer holders require a visa regardless of nationality.

cbi.program_name: Citizenship by Investment (Ciudadanía por Inversión) - Decreto 524/2025

options.notes: Investment must be classified as 'relevant' by the Ministry of Economy. Minimum investment threshold and eligible sectors not specified in the decree text - the Ministry of Economy is tasked with defining qualifying investments via separate regulation. The decree was signed July 30, 2025 and published in the Official Gazette July 31, 2025 (Boletín Oficial). Applications are submitted to the Agency for Citizenship by Investment Programs (Agencia de Programas de Ciudadanía por Inversión) under the Ministry of Economy. Security clearance required from multiple agencies (Ministry of Security, UIF, SIDE) prior to approval.

cbi.processing_time: 30 business days (from receipt of Agency report by the National Directorate of Migration)
cbi.notes: Established by Decreto 524/2025, signed by President Javier Milei on July 30, 2025. Amends Citizenship Law No. 346, adding Article 2° bis. No prior residency required - applicants qualify 'regardless of length of residence'. Investment amounts not specified in the decree; deferred to Ministry of Economy regulation. ARCA (tax authority) tasked with enabling processing of tax identification numbers for approved applicants. Official Gazette source: https://www.boletinoficial.gob.ar/detalleAviso/primera/329061/20250731

programs.name: Temporary Residence as Investor (Non-MERCOSUR) - Residencia Temporaria como Inversionista
programs.notes: For non-MERCOSUR nationals. Minimum investment of ARS 1,500,000 in a productive, commercial, or service activity. Temporary residence granted for 1 year, renewable for up to 3 years. After 2 years of residence and maintaining the investment, holders become eligible for citizenship; at year 3 eligible for permanent residency. No sector restriction. Requires title of property, securities, or partnership participation proving the effective capital investment. CAUTION: ARS 1,500,000 is subject to erosion from inflation and may not reflect current real-terms threshold - official page returned 403 on re-check and amount could not be independently re-verified as of June 2026.

programs.name: Temporary Residence as Investor (MERCOSUR) - Residencia Temporaria como Inversionista Mercosur
programs.notes: For MERCOSUR member and associate state nationals (Brazil, Paraguay, Uruguay, Bolivia, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Guyana, Peru, Suriname). Same investment requirements as non-MERCOSUR investor category. Temporary residence for 1 year, renewable up to 3 years. ARS amount subject to same inflation caveat as non-MERCOSUR program.

programs.name: Temporary Residence as Rentista (Passive Income) - Residencia Temporaria como Rentista
programs.notes: For foreign nationals with regular passive income from abroad (pensions, annuities, dividends, rents, etc.). Specific minimum income amount not confirmed from official source. Temporary residence for up to 2 years, renewable.

programs.name: Special Transitional Residency for Business/Investment Studies - Residencia Transitoria Especial para Negocios e Inversiones
programs.notes: Short-term (2 months, extendable) transitional residence for conducting business negotiations, investments, commercial representation, or market studies. Does not lead to permanent residence directly.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Transitional Residence - Residencia Transitoria como Nómada Digital
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals from countries that do not require a tourist visa to enter Argentina, who provide remote services using digital or telecommunications means to individuals or legal entities domiciled abroad
programs.processing_time: In-person process; standard migration fee schedule applies
programs.notes: Established by DNM Disposition No. 758/2022. Duration: 180 days, renewable once for an additional 180 days (total up to 12 months). Requires signed application, CV, proof of remote work activity (contracts, company endorsement, income receipts), and at least one professional reference. In-person application only; not available via the RADEX online system. Includes perks: discounts on transportation, hotels, coworking spaces, educational and cultural activities.

programs.name: Nationality Visa (MERCOSUR Members and Associates)
programs.eligibility: Nationals of MERCOSUR member or associate states (Brazil, Paraguay, Uruguay, Bolivia, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Guyana, Peru, Suriname)
programs.notes: Provides temporary establishment in Argentina for up to 2 years. Requires valid passport (6+ months validity, 2 blank pages), photographs, application form, proof of address, certificate of good conduct (age 16+), and consular fee (USD/EUR 200 equivalent). Consular interview required. Under the MERCOSUR Residency Agreement framework.

agreements: MERCOSUR (member state - common market, freedom of movement/residency framework for nationals of member and associate states)

outbound_official.notes: no official Argentina government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Argentina's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (cancilleria.gob.ar) publishes a general travel-advice page ('Requisitos de ingreso a otros países') that directs Argentine citizens to contact destination country embassies and consulates for visa requirements - it does not enumerate a country-by-country visa-free list for Argentine ordinary-passport holders. Individual mission pages (e.g. cgtel.cancilleria.gob.ar for Israel ETA, cgest.cancilleria.gob.ar for Turkey) address specific destinations in isolation but there is no centralised origin-side outbound list analogous to India's MEA vffin page.
